User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user profile in the Asnap application, managing their personal information, social connections, and cumulative earnings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

username.description: The unique username chosen by the user.

email.description: The user's email address for contact and notifications (not for authentication).

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

bio.description: A short biographical description provided by the user.

totalEarnings.description: The cumulative total earnings generated by the user from their monetized content.

followerIds.description: References to Users who follow this user. (Relationship: User N:N User)

followingIds.description: References to Users this user is following. (Relationship: User N:N User)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account information was last updated.

Post.title: Post
Post.description: Represents an ephemeral image or video post uploaded by a user, which automatically disappears after 48 hours. Video posts can generate earnings based on views.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Post entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who created the post. (Relationship: User 1:N Post)

mediaUrl.description: URL to the image or video content of the post.

mediaType.description: The type of media for the post (e.g., 'image' or 'video'). 'video' type is eligible for earnings.

caption.description: The descriptive text accompanying the post.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the post was created.

expiresAt.description: Timestamp when the post is scheduled to automatically disappear (48 hours after creation).

viewCount.description: The total number of views for this post. Essential for calculating earnings if mediaType is 'video'.

postEarnings.description: The total earnings generated by this specific post, calculated based on viewCount for video media types.

Story.title: Story
Story.description: Represents an ephemeral photo or video story shared by a user, which automatically disappears after 24 hours.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Story entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who created the story. (Relationship: User 1:N Story)

mediaUrl.description: URL to the photo or video content of the story.

mediaType.description: The type of media for the story (e.g., 'image' or 'video').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the story was created.

expiresAt.description: Timestamp when the story is scheduled to automatically disappear (24 hours after creation).

Comment.title: Comment
Comment.description: Represents a comment made by a user on a post.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Comment entity.

postId.description: Reference to the Post this comment belongs to. (Relationship: Post 1:N Comment)

userId.description: Reference to the User who made the comment. (Relationship: User 1:N Comment)

content.description: The text content of the comment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the comment was created.

Like.title: Like
Like.description: Represents a 'like' action performed by a user on a post.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Like entity.

postId.description: Reference to the Post that was liked. (Relationship: Post 1:N Like)

userId.description: Reference to the User who liked the post. (Relationship: User 1:N Like)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the like was recorded.

definition.description: Represents a user's public profile information. The `userId` corresponds to `request.auth.uid`.

Stores a user's private total earnings and other sensitive financial data. This single document ('data') ensures segregation of private data from the main user profile. Access is restricted to the user themselves for Authorization Independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Represents a user (identified by `followerId`) who follows the `userId`. Access is restricted to `userId` to view their own followers list.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user whose followers are listed.

params.name: followerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who is following `userId`.

definition.description: Represents a user (identified by `followedUserId`) that the `userId` is following. Access is restricted to `userId` to view who they follow.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who is following others.

params.name: followedUserId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user being followed by `userId`.

definition.description: Ephemeral image or video posts owned by a specific user. The `ownerId` corresponds to the `userId` in the Post entity schema. Includes denormalized `viewCount` and `postEarnings` for video posts, essential for the user's earnings dashboard.

params.name: ownerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns/created this post.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique identifier for the post.

definition.description: Ephemeral photo or video stories owned by a specific user. The `ownerId` corresponds to the `userId` in the Story entity schema.

params.name: ownerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns/created this story.

params.name: storyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the story.

definition.description: Comments on a specific post. To ensure Authorization Independence and simplify security rules, each comment document includes denormalized `postOwnerId` (the creator of the parent post) and `postExpiresAt` (when the parent post expires). The `userId` in the schema is represented by `commentCreatorId` within the document.

params.name: postOwnerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent post.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent post.

params.name: commentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the comment.

definition.description: Likes on a specific post. The `likerId` (who liked the post, corresponding to the `userId` in the schema) is used as the document ID to ensure a user can only like a post once. For Authorization Independence, each like document includes denormalized `postOwnerId` and `postExpiresAt` to avoid `get()` calls in rules.

params.name: postOwnerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent post.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent post.

params.name: likerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who liked the post (often `request.auth.uid`).

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for Asnap prioritizes **Authorization Independence** and **Clarity of Intent** by adhering strictly to denormalization mandates and structural segregation. User-specific and sensitive data, such as `totalEarnings`, is segregated into dedicated subcollections (`/users/{userId}/earnings/data`) to prevent unauthorized access when public profile data is queried. Ownership for core entities like `Post` and `Story` is explicitly embedded in hierarchical paths (`/users/{ownerId}/posts/{postId}`), which directly aligns with Firebase's best practices for user-owned data and simplifies security rules to `request.auth.uid == ownerId`.

To achieve **Authorization Independence** (CRITICAL) and eliminate the need for costly and complex `get()` calls in security rules, crucial parent attributes are denormalized into subcollection documents. For instance, `Comment` and `Like` documents within `/users/{postOwnerId}/posts/{postId}/comments/{commentId}` and `/users/{postOwnerId}/posts/{postId}/likes/{likerId}` will contain denormalized fields like `postOwnerId` and `postExpiresAt`. This allows security rules to check the parent's expiry or ownership status directly from the child document, ensuring atomic operations and simplified rule logic.

**Querying for Authorization Purposes (QAPs)** is supported efficiently:

1.  **User's Own Profile & Earnings Dashboard:** The authenticated user can easily retrieve their `totalEarnings` from `/users/{userId}/earnings/data`. For the list of videos and their earnings, they can query their own posts from `/users/{userId}/posts` with a filter for `mediaType == 'video'`. This allows for secure `list` operations directly by the owner.

2.  **Public/Follower Content Feed:** While not explicitly detailed in the request for a global feed, the structure lays the groundwork for a scalable feed. If a feed displays posts from followed users, the security rules would need to verify `request.auth.uid` is following the `postOwnerId`. If posts were directly in a global `/posts` collection, `ownerId` would be denormalized into each post document. However, by keeping posts under `/users/{ownerId}/posts`, a denormalized "feed" for each user (`/users/{viewerId}/feed/{feedPostId}`) would contain copies of posts from followed users, ensuring `list` operations for feeds remain performant and secure without complex `get()` lookups across different parent hierarchies.

3.  **Comments and Likes:** Rules for comments and likes will directly use the denormalized `postExpiresAt` and `postOwnerId` fields within their respective documents, fulfilling the QAP requirement for these nested entities.

The `Earnings` feature is integrated by storing `totalEarnings` privately in a dedicated user subcollection and `postEarnings` directly within the `Post` document. A backend service (e.g., Cloud Function) will be responsible for atomically updating `viewCount`, calculating `postEarnings`, and aggregating `totalEarnings` in a transaction to maintain data integrity.
